chunks.title: MCTC Signs Multiple Strategic Partnerships and Training Agreements (Saudi Contractors Authority Academy, Halumm, TÜV SÜD, Samsung E&A, and NEFT Energies
chunks.chunk_text:
Signing a Training Agreement with Samsung E&A

Investing in talent today builds the projects of tomorrow.
Maharat announced the signing of a major training sponsorship agreement with Samsung E&A to sponsor 300 Saudi trainees, with training programs starting in April 2026.
Announcing this partnership during our first female graduation ceremony highlights the expanding opportunities available to Saudi talent and reinforces the role of private sector collaboration in workforce transformation.
The ceremony was held under the patronage of Eng. Mansour Al-Shehri, Maharat’s Chairman of the Board of Trustees, alongside Maharat Board of Trustees members and dignitaries from the Saudi Contractors Authority, HRDF, and Saudi Aramco PMT and Training.
This is more than sponsorship — it is a long-term investment in national capability and sustainable sector growth. #FutureTalent #Localization #ConstructionCareers #SkillsForJobs #Vision2030

chunks.title: MCTC Signs a Training Agreement with Samsung E&A
chunks.chunk_text:
MCTC Signs a Training Agreement with Samsung E&A

On February 16, 2026, Maharat Construction Training Center (MCTC) signed a training agreement with Samsung E&A.

chunks.title: MCTC Signs a Training Agreement with Samsung E&A
chunks.chunk_text: On February 16, 2026, Maharat Construction Training Center (MCTC) signed a training agreement with Samsung E&A. Under this agreement, Maharat will enroll 300 trainees in its one-year diploma programs starting in April 2026, providing these young Saudis with valuable skills and hands-on experience. The partnership not only expands opportunities for national talent but also highlights the crucial role of private sector collaboration in driving workforce development and supporting the Kingdom’s broader Vision 2030 objectives for economic growth and human capital transformation.

chunks.title: MCTC Signs Multiple Strategic Partnerships and Training Agreements (Saudi Contractors Authority Academy, Halumm, TÜV SÜD, Samsung E&A, and NEFT Energies
chunks.chunk_text:
Maharat / NEFT Energies Training Services Agreement

Industry partnerships are essential to workforce excellence.
During our First Female Graduation Ceremony, Maharat marked the signing of a strategic training services
agreement with NEFT Energies during the ceremony, strengthening industry-driven professional development across Saudi Arabia.
The ceremony was held under the patronage of Eng. Mansour Al-Shehri, Maharat’s Chairman of the Board of Trustees, alongside Maharat Board of Trustees members and dignitaries from the Saudi Contractors Authority, HRDF, and Saudi Aramco PMT and Training.
The collaboration will deliver specialized programs in:
✔ Health, Safety, Security & Environment (HSSE)
✔ Technical construction disciplines
✔ Leadership and professional skills development
We were honored by the presence of NEFT Energies leadership:
- Mr. Bashaar A. Alidi – CEO
- Mr. Mohammad Gamal – Regional Operations Manager
- Mr. Abdulaziz Jawed – Business Development
This partnership reflects a shared commitment to developing highly skilled professionals ready to support the Kingdom’s expanding industrial and construction sectors.
#HSSE #ProfessionalDevelopment #TechnicalTraining #WorkforceUpskilling #Vision2030

chunks.title: MCTC Signs Multiple Strategic Partnerships and Training Agreements (Saudi Contractors Authority Academy, Halumm, TÜV SÜD, Samsung E&A, and NEFT Energies
chunks.chunk_text:
Maharat / Halumm Innovation Partnership

Innovation is transforming construction — and workforce development must evolve with it.
Maharat signed a strategic MoU with Halumm Construction Innovation Company during the First Female Graduation Ceremony on 16 February 2026 at Ithra Cinema.
The ceremony was held under the patronage of Eng. Mansour Al-Shehri, Maharat’s Chairman of the Board of Trustees, alongside Maharat Board of Trustees members and dignitaries from the Saudi Contractors Authority, HRDF, and Saudi Aramco PMT and Training.
The MoU outlines a strategic collaboration focused on innovation-driven workforce development and modernization of the construction sector. The partnership includes joint applied training programs in industrialized and prefabricated construction, industry immersion opportunities, development of scalable training systems, and potential collaboration on international standards development, including steel prefabricated construction technologies. The cooperation also supports large-scale workforce training initiatives aligned with Saudi Vision 2030.
We were honored to welcome halumm leadership: Mr. Xiaodong Wang – Chairman, and Ms. Yurong Li – President.
By combining innovation with skills development, this partnership supports the modernization of the Kingdom’s construction sector and prepares the next generation of professionals for emerging technologies.
#ConstructionInnovation #Prefabrication #FutureOfConstruction #IndustrializedConstruction #Vision2030

chunks.title: MCTC Signs Multiple Strategic Partnerships and Training Agreements (Saudi Contractors Authority Academy, Halumm, TÜV SÜD, Samsung E&A, and NEFT Energies
chunks.chunk_text:
Maharat / Saudi Contractors Authority Academy Partnership, Halumm Innovation Partnership,

Strong partnerships build stronger industries.
Maharat proudly signed a strategic MoU with the Saudi Contractors Authority (SCA) Academy during our First Female Graduation Ceremony.
This collaboration will drive sector advancement through:
✔ Joint professional certifications
✔ Industry-aligned training programs
✔ Curriculum & standards development
✔ Sector research and knowledge initiatives
✔ Enhanced training facility collaboration
We were privileged to host distinguished SCA Academy leadership: Mr. Mohammed Alajalan – SCA Chairman of the Board, Mr. Saud Al Aqidi – SCA Board Member and Maharat’s BoT member, Mr. Abdulmajeed Alrashoudi – SCA Governor, Mr. Abdulaziz Almehaish SCA Eastern Province Branch Manager, and Mr. Yazeed Aldossary SCA Committees Manager.
This partnership represents a major step toward building a future-ready Saudi contracting sector powered by skilled national talent.
#ContractingSector #TVET #SkillsDevelopment #ConstructionLeadership #Vision2030

chunks.title: MCTC Hosts Visit from Anmar Training Center
chunks.chunk_text: On January 12, 2026, the Training Manager of Anmar International Training Center in Jubail visited MCTC. During the visit, he met with MCTC’s Academic Training Supervisor to discuss potential avenues of collaboration between the two institutions. The visitor expressed his willingness to accommodate Maharat trainees at his training center and offered to provide manpower support if needed. He also emphasized that a partnership between the two institutes would support the achievement of training goals and initiatives for both organizations. This visit serves as a strong testament to the positive reputation Maharat has built within the training and vocational education community over the past few years.

chunks.title: MCTC Signs Multiple Strategic MoUs
chunks.chunk_text: On February 16, 2026, during Maharat’s First Female Graduation Ceremony held under the patronage of Eng. Mansour Al-Shehri, Maharat’s Chairman of the Board of Trustees, Maharat proudly signed strategic MoUs with the Saudi Contractors Authority Academy, Halumm, TÜV SÜD, and NEFT Energies. These partnerships represent key steps toward building a future-ready Saudi contracting sector powered by skilled national talent. They advance the modernization of the Kingdom’s construction industry by combining innovation with skills development, preparing the next generation for emerging technologies, raising professional standards, and promoting safer workplaces. Collectively, they underscore a vision for a construction sector that is safer, smarter, and more inclusive.
